takeaways: Senior housing occupancy is on track to break 90% in 2026 and could match the all-time 2007 record of 91.3%, as inventory growth sits at its lowest level since 2006 and the oldest boomers turn 80.

A $1.5T CRE debt-maturity wall collides with renewed investor appetite — Colliers forecasts a 15–20% jump in 2026 sales volume even as the 10Y Treasury sticks near 4.1% and office CMBS delinquencies top 12%.
AI moves from pilot to production in CRE: the share of firms running AI initiatives jumped from 5% to 92% in three years, with proptech VC hitting $16.7B in 2025 (+68% YoY).

items.headline: Senior housing occupancy nears historic 91.3% record in 2026
items.summary: Industry leaders expect occupancy to cross the 90% threshold this year and potentially match the 2007 all-time high of 91.3%, as the oldest boomers turn 80 and year-over-year inventory growth fell to its lowest level since 2006. For Lloyd Jones, the tight supply backdrop supports rent power and stabilization upside on existing assets.

items.headline: Harrison Street pegs 2026 senior housing rent growth at 3–6%
items.summary: Harrison Street expects senior housing fundamentals to stay strong in 2026, with rent growth running 3–6% — active adult at the low end, higher-acuity units in supply-constrained markets pushing toward the top. The range gives operators headroom to offset stubborn labor costs.

items.headline: $1.5T in CRE loans maturing through 2026 forces refi reckoning
items.summary: Roughly $1.5 trillion of commercial real estate loans mature by year-end 2026, with average borrowing costs near 6.57% running tight to average cap rates of 6.34%. Sponsors with senior housing assets need to model rate-cap and equity gap scenarios now ahead of refi windows.

items.headline: Colliers forecasts 15–20% jump in 2026 CRE sales volume
items.summary: Colliers projects a 15–20% increase in commercial real estate sales volume in 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital reenter the market. Renewed transaction flow improves comps and exit visibility for Lloyd Jones dispositions and recapitalizations.

items.headline: 92% of CRE firms now run AI pilots, up from 5% in three years
items.summary: The share of commercial real estate companies running AI pilots has surged from 5% to 92% in just three years, marking AI's shift from experimentation into operational dependence. Owner-operators that don't have an AI roadmap by Q3 risk falling behind on underwriting speed and asset-level productivity.

items.headline: Proptech VC hits $16.7B in 2025, up 68% with AI-native leading
items.summary: Venture investment in proptech reached $16.7B in 2025, up 68% year-over-year, with AI-native companies capturing $4.5B and growing their share of proptech VC dollars 42% YoY. Expect a wave of senior-housing-specific tools — leasing, RCM, staffing — hitting the market through year-end.

items.headline: 89% of employees perform better when wellbeing is prioritized
items.summary: Wellhub's 2026 State of Work-Life-Wellness report finds 89% of employees report higher performance when employers prioritize wellbeing, framing wellness as one of the highest-ROI talent strategies available. Directly relevant to senior housing's persistent caregiver and dining-staff retention problem.

items.headline: Mastercard reshuffles C-suite to sharpen execution in 2026
items.summary: Mastercard announced leadership changes designed to accelerate global growth and deepen customer focus, including Ling Hai — president of Asia Pacific, Europe, Middle East and Africa — moving into the CFO seat to succeed Sachin Mehra. A reminder that even high-performing operators are rewiring leadership around regional execution.

items.headline: CRE rates run 5–12.75% as 10Y Treasury holds near 4.1%
items.summary: Commercial loan rates currently span 5.04%–12.75%, with conventional CRE mortgages pricing 5.35%–8.75% depending on property type and sponsor credit, while the 10Y Treasury benchmark — which drives mortgage pricing more than the Fed funds rate — has stuck near 4.1%. Underwriting senior housing acquisitions should assume rates stay range-bound through 2H.

items.headline: Office CMBS delinquencies top 12% as other sectors hold
items.summary: Office CMBS delinquencies have pushed past 12% — a record — while industrial and multifamily continue to perform. The bifurcation strengthens the relative case for capital flowing into resilient sectors including senior housing.

items.headline: Workforce shortage remains top senior living concern in 2026
items.summary: Ensuring enough caregivers, dining staff and housekeepers continues to top the senior living industry's concern list as the older adult population swells. Operators are responding with structural wage repricing and earlier-in-the-funnel recruiting — Lloyd Jones budgets should reflect persistent labor inflation.

items.headline: Agemark staffs up ahead of next senior living growth wave
items.summary: Operator Agemark is adding headcount across its corporate and field teams in anticipation of the next senior living growth cycle, signaling that peer operators are betting on demand absorbing the limited new supply. The buildup highlights how operators are positioning for a multi-year tailwind.

items.headline: Agentic AI is the next phase of proptech, ICSC says
items.summary: ICSC frames agentic AI — autonomous agents that decide and execute, not just generate — as the next major proptech phase, with retailers and real estate operators already deploying agents for procurement, pricing, inventory and event programming. Senior housing operators should pilot agentic workflows in leasing and supply procurement first.

items.headline: AI delivers 30% energy savings, up to 30% OpEx cuts in CRE
items.summary: Early enterprise adoption of AI in real estate operations is delivering measurable results: up to 30% energy savings and 15–30% reductions in operating costs through automation and AI-driven workflows. NOI uplift of that size moves valuations meaningfully on stabilized senior housing assets.

items.headline: JPMorgan: 73% of leaders expect revenue growth in 2026
items.summary: JPMorgan's 2026 Business Leaders Outlook finds 73% of leaders anticipate higher revenue and 64% expect higher profits — both nearly unchanged from a year ago — while 49% flag uncertain economic conditions as a top concern. Investor sentiment is holding, but capex plans are being staged.

items.headline: Human + AI leadership emerges as defining 2026 trend
items.summary: DDI and World Business Forum both name human + AI leadership the defining management trend of 2026, alongside flatter org structures, rising burnout, and stalled internal mobility. Operators that pair empathic management with AI-augmented decision-making will outperform on retention.

items.headline: Emotional fitness emerges as new wellness category in 2026
items.summary: Global wellness research is naming "emotional fitness" a new health category for 2026, focused on cognitive performance and emotional resilience alongside physical wellness. Senior housing programming that integrates emotional fitness for residents and staff can differentiate at lease-up.

items.headline: AI-driven personalized wellness goes mainstream in 2026
items.summary: Hyper-personal, data-driven wellness — biomarker-based nutrition, AI wellness coaches, at-home diagnostics — is hitting mainstream adoption in 2026. Senior living amenity stacks are an obvious early-adopter category for residents and as a recruiting differentiator for staff.

items.headline: "Quiet cracking" and burnout rising across workforces
items.summary: DDI flags "quiet cracking" — a step beyond quiet quitting where employees show visible signs of disengagement and exhaustion — as a top 2026 leadership challenge. Frontline-heavy industries like senior housing are most exposed and need active intervention, not just engagement surveys.

items.headline: June is prime window for outdoor and social wellness programming
items.summary: Wellness programmers point to June — longer days, warmer weather, natural energy lift — as the best window of the year to drive social connection and outdoor movement, two engagement drivers that drop off in hybrid environments. A timely cue for community-level programming in senior housing assets.
